mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate signals monetary tightening that lifts the discount rate applied to equities and reduces risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative when the policy rate is increasing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in a majority of months with a positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which depresses equity valuations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative returns, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS reflects stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue momentum for consumer firms, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through earnings-expectation revision.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly or 4-week changes in RSAFS do not precede above-median 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the estimated effect is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in INDPRO and HOUST signals broad real-sector production and construction momentum, which should support cyclical earnings expectations and raise S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST is not positive and significant, or if high-growth joint observations fail to outperform all other states on 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, the market interprets the combination as rising future policy tightening risk, which lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50%
regime_hint: reflation scare with near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-low-rate trigger does not coincide with negative excess 20-day returns, or if CPI acceleration alone explains the effect without FEDFUNDS interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates payroll contraction, weaker income growth, and lower future consumption, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative after confirmed job losses.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines are followed by positive median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broad market trend in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with falling RSAFS indicates broad demand deceleration, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below 1% annualized and RSAFS 3-month growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDP-plus-negative-retail states do not underperform all other states on 60-day returns, or if either series alone fully subsumes the joint effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock industrial rebound, rising INDPRO with stable or declining UNRATE signals improving capacity utilization and better earnings leverage, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes do not predict positive 20-day returns when UNRATE is flat or falling, or if the joint state underperforms the unconditional benchmark in rolling out-of-sample tests.
